Maalavidaa.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Maalavidaa is the persona of Alycia Rainaud, French graphic designer and digital artist based in Montréal, QC, Canada; brand/persona founded 2016. Education: Master’s graphic design DSAA Lycée Denis Diderot Marseille (2018, thesis on psychology in design); prior graphic design training ESDAC Aix-en-Provence. Practice ties abstract neon aesthetics to Jungian therapy, color therapy, meditation; founder of Heal The Deal (2021) and Creative Director at 0x Society (verify titles/dates on site).
Maalavidaa.pressText:
The Art Investor — — Digital art / practice feature — https://theartinvestor.co.uk/digital-art/how-alycia-rainaud-of-maalavidaa-creates-a-neon-wildness-of-nature-and-f/
SHOUTOUT LA — — Interview — https://shoutoutla.com/meet-alycia-rainaud-aka-maalavidaa-graphic-designer-digital-artist/

Psoman.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Psoman / Pso Man—born 1981, Brussels area (Liège region per tour writeups). Graphic design studies Belgium 2001; further 3D/welding/polyester training Switzerland. Muralist, painter, sculptor, illustrator; themes of nature, climate, urbanization; work in STRAAT Museum Amsterdam collection and Artsy listing. Active in festivals across Europe and Asia since 2001 (self-reported—confirm on psoman.co).
Psoman.pressText:
STRAAT Museum — — Collection database: Fallin’ Apart — https://straatmuseum.com/en/collection-database/pso-man-fallin-apart
Artsy — — Artist page — https://www.artsy.net/artist/psoman

Parcours Street Art Brussels — — Artist page — https://parcoursstreetart.brussels/artistes/pso-man/

Agus Rucula.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Argentine urban artist, teacher, illustrator; street practice from ~2012 while studying; full-time urban art ~2017. Training: Polivalente de Arte San Isidro; FADU UBA Industrial Design; additional workshops. Human figure and gesture; community-oriented walls; festivals in Argentina, Colombia, Mexico, Ecuador, Bolivia, Brazil, Uruguay, Spain (VenUS Urban Art).
Agus Rucula.pressText:
VenUS Urban Art — — Artist page — https://www.venusurbanart.com/agus-rucula
Murales Buenos Aires — 2018 — Interview — https://muralesbuenosaires.com.ar/2018/12/11/entrevista-a-agus-rucula/
LINK Urban Art Festival — — Festival artist page — https://linkurbanartfestival.it/agus-rucula/

Max Diamond.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Poster artist and designer active as @maxdiamond52 (Instagram) and MaxDiamond on Foundation; work includes Severance (Apple TV) fan/poster pieces and retro sci-fi album-style art per public posts and profiles. Commercial credits require separate verification; treat Instagram/Foundation as primary public sources.

Ezra Baderman.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): British-Israeli multidisciplinary artist; born Paris, raised London; based Lisbon (per about copy—verify). Education: John Cass Art School foundation; Bezalel fine art and visual communications. Practice: collage, painting, installation, street-poster salvage in Tel Aviv, mixed media (see site).
Ezra Baderman.exhibitionsText: 2020 — Solo, Chaos and Order, Salon Bagdad, Tel Aviv, Israel (curated Ellada Ilana Matatov; verify dates)
Ezra Baderman.pressText:
Secret Tel Aviv — 2020 — Chaos and Order solo exhibition listing — https://www.secrettelaviv.com/tickets/ezra-baderman-chaos-and-order-solo-exhibition
Saatchi Art — — Artist profile — https://www.saatchiart.com/ebaderman

Marylou Faure.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): French illustrator/artist from Paris; MA art direction/graphic design ESAG Peninghen; moved to London 2012. Clients cited include Coca-Cola, Kiehl’s, YouTube, Levi’s, MTV, McLaren F1, and others (self-reported—verify). Known for bold character design and feminist-leaning figurative work (Design Week profile).
Marylou Faure.exhibitionsText:
2024 — Solo, It's All About Me, E03, Beijing, China
2024 — Solo, Come Join Me, L44, Sofia, Bulgaria
2022 — Solo, Skinny Dipping, Cuemars, London, UK
2021 — Solo, Blossom, Woodbury House x Nicce, London, UK
2020 — Solo, À La Piscine, The Book Club, London, UK
2020 — Solo, Daisies, Hen's Teeth, Dublin, Ireland
2019 — Solo, Nobody's Baby, The Print Space, London, UK
2019 — Solo, Nobody's Baby, Slow Gallerie, Paris, France

Dima Korma.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): Visual artist/illustrator based in Berlin; originally from Israel. Visual communication studies at Shenkar. Abstract compositions, typography, murals; projects cited in Berlin gallery materials and street-art booking profiles (Barcelona, Chemnitz, Tampa, etc.—verify each). Walls of Hope mural project mention (wallsofhope.net).

Galleri Heike Arndt Berlin — — Artist page — https://berlin.heike-arndt.dk/dk/dima-korma-il
Book a Street Artist — — Profile — https://www.bookastreetartist.com/dima-korma
Walls of Hope — — Project mention — https://wallsofhope.net/dima-korma/

Samme Snow.additionalHistoryText: Web research (verify): London-based illustrator; Graphic Communication degree Farnham, 2013. Murals across London, Oxford, Manchester; clients include Canary Wharf, House of Vans, GQ Middle East, and others (site list—verify). Since 2020 emphasis on community workshops; Colouring Workshops from 2021 reaching 6000+ participants (self-reported).
Samme Snow.exhibitionsText: 2025 — Workshop residency, Secret 7" Colouring Experience, NOW Gallery, Greenwich Peninsula, London, UK (May–Jun; verify)
